name: Misawa Air Base
tagline: A unique joint U.S.-Japanese air base in northern Japan, home of the 35th Fighter Wing.
_note: True OCONUS (Japan). OHA housing, DoDEA Pacific schools, command-sponsorship/SOFA notes, no BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger/rent-buy sections (US-only feeds). USAF-led 35th Fighter Wing (F-16); a unique combined base shared by USAF, US Navy, US Army, and the Japan Air Self-Defense Force. Northern Honshu (Aomori Prefecture); cold/snowy winters. Medical: 35th Medical Group clinic. Facts verified from official sources (misawa.af.mil, 35 MDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, DoDEA Pacific, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx).

data.Branch: U.S. Air Force (joint, and shared with Japan)
data.Unique: A combined base shared by U.S. Air Force, Navy, Army, and the Japan Air Self-Defense Force
data.Climate: Four seasons with cold, snowy winters

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Misawa Air Base is home to the U.S. Air Force's 35th Fighter Wing, flying F-16s, and is unique as a combined installation shared by the U.S. Air Force, Navy, and Army alongside the Japan Air Self-Defense Force.

It sits in Aomori Prefecture on the northern end of Honshu, a region of four distinct seasons, including cold, snowy winters. The tight-knit base community and authentic rural-Japan setting make it a memorable overseas tour.

sections.title: A Northern Japan Tour: On-Base vs. Living Off Base

data.subtitle: Misawa is a beloved, community-focused overseas tour. The main choice is on-base housing versus living off base in the local Japanese community with OHA.
intro: Most families come command-sponsored. The base community is famously tight-knit, and on-base housing is a common, easy choice for a northern-Japan tour with snowy winters.
Living off base in Misawa city or nearby means deeper immersion in Japanese life, using the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA), balanced against a Japanese lease, the language, and winter driving.

recommendations.advice: On-base housing keeps you close to work, DoDEA schools, and services, ideal for a first overseas tour and easier through the snowy winters.
Renting off base in Misawa offers a deeper Japanese experience with OHA to offset rent.
The housing office assigns on-base housing and must approve off-base rentals, and helps with leases and OHA, so start there.
Misawa has real winters with great skiing nearby; lean into the snow and the local festivals.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Access follows U.S. and host-nation (SOFA) rules. Visitors without a DoD ID need to be sponsored and registered through the Visitor Control Center.
Requirements for overseas installations can be stricter and change with force-protection levels, so confirm the current details on the official Misawa Air Base site before you go.

sections.title: Housing (On-Base & OHA)
data: Misawa has on-base family housing, and many families instead live off base in the local community, receiving the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) to offset rent and utilities rather than the stateside BAH.
The housing office assigns on-base housing and must approve off-base rentals; it also helps with leases, utilities, and OHA. Start there as soon as you have orders.

sections.title: Schools (DoDEA)
data: Children of U.S. personnel attend Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) schools on base, serving students from elementary through high school.
Confirm current schools, boundaries, and registration through DoDEA Pacific and the base, and bring records and immunizations when you register.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and specialty care on base; inpatient and complex care via larger military hospitals in Japan and host-nation facilities

intro: An overseas PCS to Japan has extra steps beyond a stateside move, and northern Japan adds winter prep. Get these sorted early.

steps.title: Confirm command sponsorship
steps.text: Make sure your family is command-sponsored before or upon arrival; it drives your OHA, DoDEA enrollment, and travel entitlements.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, update DEERS, and get overseas ID and logistics cards as required.

steps.title: Sort housing and OHA
steps.text: See the housing office first. Choose on-base housing or get approval and help to rent off base with OHA; claim Temporary Lodging Allowance (TLA) while you look.

steps.title: Handle SOFA, license, and vehicle
steps.text: Get your SOFA driver's license, register a vehicle locally (many families buy a used car in Japan), and prep for snowy winter driving. Confirm whether family members need a no-fee (official) passport.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Register with the DoDEA Misawa schools early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Enroll in the TRICARE Overseas Program so you can be seen at the 35th Medical Group clinic, with larger Japan military hospitals and host-nation providers for specialty care.

data.q: Do I get BAH at Misawa?
data.a: No. Overseas you receive the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) for off-base rentals, or you live in on-base housing. OHA is based on your actual rent up to a cap, not the stateside BAH tables.
It's a combined base shared by the U.S. Air Force, Navy, and Army alongside the Japan Air Self-Defense Force, home to the 35th Fighter Wing flying F-16s.
Four real seasons, including cold, snowy winters in northern Japan, with skiing nearby. Plan for winter driving.
Kids attend DoDEA schools on base, from elementary through high school.
